headline: Agentic AI moves beyond marketing into autonomous enterprise operations
body: Hershey is deploying agentic AI to optimize $2 billion in marketing spend, signaling broader enterprise adoption of autonomous AI agents that can act independently on business objectives. For healthcare systems, this pattern matters because similar agentic approaches are entering clinical workflows and operational decision-making, from prior authorization automation to supply chain management. The velocity of this trend (86 score, 10 unique sources) suggests healthcare vendors and consultants need to prepare for client demands around autonomous agents handling sensitive decisions. Unlike traditional AI tools that require human approval at each step, agentic systems operate with programmed autonomy, raising critical questions about governance, accountability, and safety in clinical settings where wrong decisions carry patient safety implications.
